page: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 1 physical core supporting 2 threads, resulting in 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. This local storage provides direct, low-latency disk access. The network configuration delivers a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and high throughput in single-threaded operations like gzip compression at 33.96 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.43 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks show limitations. The multi-core Geekbench score of 2695.0 and stress-ng multi-core performance fall into the lower percentiles, reflecting the constraints of a single physical core. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also relatively weak, measuring 22.4 GB/sec for read operations.

From a resource density perspective, the m8id.large offers a qualitative balance of high-speed DDR5 memory and local NVMe storage on a dual-vCPU footprint. It is best suited for workloads that prioritize single-threaded CPU performance and local scratch space over heavy multi-threaded parallelization. Suitable applications include lightweight web serving, single-threaded text processing, basic database operations, and development environments that benefit from fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are limited due to the dual-vCPU design. This instance provides a balanced, entry-level hardware profile for workloads requiring fast local storage and strong single-thread execution, such as lightweight web serving, text processing, and development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and 118 GB NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, local NVMe SSD storage, and strong single-core Intel Xeon performance.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz
8.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
118 GB of low-latency local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Optimized for lightweight databases, web serving, and single-threaded tasks
